chapter_context.summary: A second council permits Satan to afflict Job's body with boils; Job resists his wife's urge to curse God; Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar arrive and sit in seven days of silent mourning.

outline.text: The Second Heavenly Council; Satan Allowed to Strike Job's Body
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Second Heavenly Council; Satan Allowed to Strike Job's Body (2:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Job.

outline.text: Job Afflicted with Boils; Resists His Wife's Advice to Curse God
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Job Afflicted with Boils; Resists His Wife's Advice to Curse God (2:7-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Job.

outline.text: Arrival of Job's Three Friends; Seven Days of Silence
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Arrival of Job's Three Friends; Seven Days of Silence (2:11-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Job.

key_themes.title: The Mystery of Suffering
key_themes.description: Job demonstrates that suffering is not always a direct consequence of personal sin, challenging simplistic retributive theology.

key_themes.title: Divine Sovereignty
key_themes.description: God's answer from the whirlwind exalts His absolute power, wisdom, and control over all creation, far exceeding human understanding.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 2, how can you remain trusting in God when circumstances are incomprehensible and painful?
How does this chapter prompt you to surrender your demands for explanation and trust in the absolute wisdom of the Creator?

cultural_historical.title: The Land of Uz and Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Job is set in the land of Uz and belongs to the Wisdom Literature genre of the Hebrew Bible, exploring profound philosophical and theological questions.
